audit_items.proof_trace: Solady v0.0.182 mulDivUp (L543-553) performs an explicit 256-bit overflow check (`gt(x, div(not(0), y))`) and reverts with MulDivFailed() if amountIn * assetInUsdPrice would exceed type(uint256).max — no silent corruption is possible. Additionally, bid() bounds amountIn to IERC20(asset).balanceOf(address(this)) (L438), constraining the operand to the contract's actual token holdings. For any realistic token supply and oracle price (e.g., 10^33 raw supply × 10^28 Chainlink price ≈ 10^61 << 2^256 ≈ 10^77), the overflow boundary is unreachable in practice.
